intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only pot showing a remnant of biological material, though it is in a state of severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot; contains only soil and debris.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot; contains the white rabbit anchor and a single, chlorotic, drooping leaf. The leaf shows signs of necrosis.
plant_audit.P4: Shared with P2; no distinct plant structure observed.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3; electronic component (IC/chip) present on the desk surface in earlier frames, removed in later frames.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Electronic component remains on desk; P3 leaf shows slight postural shift.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Electronic component removed; P3 leaf continues to droop.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant lighting drop; P3 leaf appears to have lost turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Total darkness/low light; P3 leaf is barely visible, indicating near-total collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal collapse. The lack of light, combined with the absence of viable foliage and the presence of necrotic tissue in P3, suggests the plants have succumbed to environmental stress, likely lack of light and potential desiccation.
anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments (likely a calcium supplement attempt or debris).
Electronic component (IC) on desk surface in early frames (New Intervention/Removal).
Severe light deprivation in later frames.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Starting from the earliest image, the plants were already in a state of extreme neglect. The Pothos (P3) was the last remaining specimen, which has progressively lost its structural integrity and vitality. The removal of the electronic component suggests human intervention, but this has not mitigated the biological decline. The current state is essentially a graveyard of pots.
